files_not_modified_but_relevant: engine/catalog/catalog.go (CompareAndSwapCurrentVersion — signature unchanged, called only from the new commit-lock-serialized path)
engine/catalog/recovery.go (RecoverFromWAL — reused as-is, no version-CAS-specific record type needed)
breaking_changes: VersionWriter.CommitVersion gains a new *wal.Writer parameter (2nd positional arg): CommitVersion(cat *catalog.Catalog, w *wal.Writer, fileID uint64, data []byte) (uint64, error). Anticipated by 2a.1.2's verification handoff (CommitVersion explicitly not WAL-safe / not to be used by real callers until this subtask).
callers_updated: engine/mvcc/write_test.go: TestCurrentVersionCAS (adds wal.Writer to test fixture)
engine/mvcc/read_test.go: TestSnapshotRead (adds wal.Writer to test fixture)
no_production_callers_yet: CommitVersion has no real (non-test) callers in the repo yet; this subtask closes the WAL-safety gap so a future caller-wiring subtask CAN use it safely.
regression_risk_areas: engine/mvcc/read.go Snapshot/Read path (2a.1.3) — depends only on cat.Get + VersionPath, unaffected by CommitVersion's internal WAL wiring

engine/catalog RecoverFromWAL / ContentStore Create/Append — untouched, but new WAL records now also flow through the same WAL directory when CommitVersion is used against a real wal.Writer; no shared state with ContentStore's own WAL usage beyond the Writer type itself
